root_causes.type: Configuration error - routing
root_causes.cause: Reciprocal static/next-hop routes for 172.16.0.0/24 on R1 and R3 cause packets for 172.16.0.12 to be forwarded back-and-forth (blackholed) instead of reaching the destination.
evidence: R1 interface Serial0/1/1 ip = 10.0.0.10 (description: Link to R3)
R3 interface Serial0/1/0 ip = 10.0.0.9 (description: Link to R1)

root_causes.type: Missing/incorrect route advertisement or next-hop for destination network
root_causes.cause: No evidence that the 172.16.0.0/24 network is locally attached to either R1 or R3; intended path via 'router2' may be absent or not being advertised/used, so 172.16.0.12 is not reachable via a correct next-hop.
evidence: Topology shows R1 and R3 also linked to 'router2' (R1 Serial0/1/0 description 'Link to R2', R3 Serial0/1/1 description 'Link to R2') but neither R1 nor R3 have a route to 172.16.0.0 that points at router2.
Attempted fetch for router2 running-config failed: "All connection attempts failed" (no config available to confirm routing/advertisement)

data.intent_description: Fix routing so 172.16.0.12 is reachable from 192.168.0.12 by removing reciprocal static next-hops between R1 and R3 and pointing the 172.16.0.0/24 route to router2 (so traffic is forwarded toward the correct router instead of being blackholed).

plan.problem: Reciprocal static routes on router1 and router3 for 172.16.0.0/24 forward packets to each other (10.0.0.9 <-> 10.0.0.10), causing reachability loss to 172.16.0.12. Missing confirmation that router2 advertises/hosts 172.16.0.0/24; remediation assumes router2 is the correct next-hop and is reachable at 10.0.0.11. Missing info: router2 link IPs and router2 routing/advertisement state.
plan.fix_summary: Remove incorrect static routes that point 172.16.0.0/24 at the opposite router, and add static routes on R1 and R3 pointing 172.16.0.0/24 to router2 (assumed next-hop 10.0.0.11).

R1 interface Serial0/1/1 ip = 10.0.0.10/30 (description: Link to R3)

router2 running-config fetch error = All connection attempts failed

evidence: R1 running-config shows a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.11 (get_running_config for R1).
R3 running-config shows a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.11 (get_running_config for R3).
Both R1 and R3 no longer point 172.16.0.0/24 at each other (previously 10.0.0.9 <-> 10.0.0.10); they now point at 10.0.0.11, eliminating the reciprocal next-hop blackhole.
remaining_issues: No confirmation that router2 actually hosts or advertises 172.16.0.0/24 — pointing R1/R3 at 10.0.0.11 fixes the routing-loop/blackhole but we cannot confirm the destination network is present on router2.
router2 running-config fetch previously failed (All connection attempts failed); router2 state remains unknown.
missing_info: Running-config (or equivalent) from router2 to confirm it has IP 10.0.0.11 on the R1/R3-facing interface and that 172.16.0.0/24 is locally attached or being advertised.
If possible, a routing table or static route on router2 showing reachability to 172.16.0.0/24 (or evidence that router2 will forward traffic to 172.16.0.12).

evidence: R1 running-config shows a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.11 (get_running_config for R1).
R3 running-config shows a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.11 (get_running_config for R3).
Both R1 and R3 no longer point 172.16.0.0/24 at each other (previously 10.0.0.9 <-> 10.0.0.10); they now point at 10.0.0.11, eliminating the reciprocal next-hop blackhole.
remaining_issues: No confirmation that router2 actually hosts or advertises 172.16.0.0/24 — pointing R1/R3 at 10.0.0.11 fixes the routing-loop/blackhole but we cannot confirm the destination network is present on router2.
router2 running-config fetch previously failed (All connection attempts failed); router2 state remains unknown.
missing_info: Running-config (or equivalent) from router2 to confirm it has IP 10.0.0.11 on the R1/R3-facing interface and that 172.16.0.0/24 is locally attached or being advertised.
If possible, a routing table or static route on router2 showing reachability to 172.16.0.0/24 (or evidence that router2 will forward traffic to 172.16.0.12).

data.intent_description: Fix routing so 172.16.0.12 is reachable from 192.168.0.12 by removing reciprocal static next-hops between R1 and R3 and pointing the 172.16.0.0/24 route to router2 (so traffic is forwarded toward the correct router instead of being blackholed).

evidence.detail: R1 Serial 0/1/0 description 'Link to R2', ip 10.0.0.1/30
R3 Serial 0/1/1 description 'Link to R2', ip 10.0.0.6/30

recent_changes.summary: Fetched running-config for R1; extracted hostname, ios_xe_version, interfaces and serial links.
Fetched running-config for R3; extracted hostname, ios_xe_version, loopback and serial links.
Attempted fetch for router2 failed (no running-config).

facts.claim: R1 interface Serial0/1/0 ip = 10.0.0.1/30 (description: Link to R2)
R1 interface Serial0/1/1 ip = 10.0.0.10/30 (description: Link to R3)
R3 interface Serial0/1/0 ip = 10.0.0.9/30 (description: Link to R1)
R3 interface Serial0/1/1 ip = 10.0.0.6/30 (description: Link to R2)
router2 running-config fetch error = All connection attempts failed

root_causes.type: Configuration error - routing
root_causes.cause: Reciprocal static/next-hop routes for 172.16.0.0/24 on R1 and R3 cause packets for 172.16.0.12 to be forwarded back-and-forth (blackholed) instead of reaching the destination.
evidence: R1 interface Serial0/1/1 ip = 10.0.0.10 (description: Link to R3)
R3 interface Serial0/1/0 ip = 10.0.0.9 (description: Link to R1)

root_causes.type: Missing/incorrect route advertisement or next-hop for destination network
root_causes.cause: No evidence that the 172.16.0.0/24 network is locally attached to either R1 or R3; intended path via 'router2' may be absent or not being advertised/used, so 172.16.0.12 is not reachable via a correct next-hop.
evidence: Topology shows R1 and R3 also linked to 'router2' (R1 Serial0/1/0 description 'Link to R2', R3 Serial0/1/1 description 'Link to R2') but neither R1 nor R3 have a route to 172.16.0.0 that points at router2.
Attempted fetch for router2 running-config failed: "All connection attempts failed" (no config available to confirm routing/advertisement)

plan.problem: Reciprocal static routes on router1 and router3 for 172.16.0.0/24 forward packets to each other (10.0.0.9 <-> 10.0.0.10), causing reachability loss to 172.16.0.12. Missing confirmation that router2 advertises/hosts 172.16.0.0/24; remediation assumes router2 is the correct next-hop and is reachable at 10.0.0.11. Missing info: router2 link IPs and router2 routing/advertisement state.
plan.fix_summary: Remove incorrect static routes that point 172.16.0.0/24 at the opposite router, and add static routes on R1 and R3 pointing 172.16.0.0/24 to router2 (assumed next-hop 10.0.0.11).

evidence: R1 running-config shows a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.11 (get_running_config for R1).
R3 running-config shows a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.11 (get_running_config for R3).
Both R1 and R3 no longer point 172.16.0.0/24 at each other (previously 10.0.0.9 <-> 10.0.0.10); they now point at 10.0.0.11, eliminating the reciprocal next-hop blackhole.
remaining_issues: No confirmation that router2 actually hosts or advertises 172.16.0.0/24 — pointing R1/R3 at 10.0.0.11 fixes the routing-loop/blackhole but we cannot confirm the destination network is present on router2.
router2 running-config fetch previously failed (All connection attempts failed); router2 state remains unknown.
missing_info: Running-config (or equivalent) from router2 to confirm it has IP 10.0.0.11 on the R1/R3-facing interface and that 172.16.0.0/24 is locally attached or being advertised.
If possible, a routing table or static route on router2 showing reachability to 172.16.0.0/24 (or evidence that router2 will forward traffic to 172.16.0.12).

messages.content:
What was observed
- R1 and R3 had reciprocal static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 (R1 -> 10.0.0.9, R3 -> 10.0.0.10) causing packets to be forwarded between them and effectively blackholed.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Configuration error: reciprocal static/next-hop routes on R1 and R3 causing a routing blackhole instead of forwarding toward the correct router (router2).

Changes made
- On R1: removed the static 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9 and added 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.11.
- On R3: removed the static 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.10 and added 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.11.

Verification
- Passed (configuration-level): R1 and R3 running-configs now show static route 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.11; they no longer point at each other.
- Remaining issues: router2 could not be contacted earlier (running-config fetch failed), so there is no confirmation that router2 actually owns/advertises 172.16.0.0/24 or that 10.0.0.11 is present. End-to-end reachability to 172.16.0.12 is not yet confirmed.

Recommended next steps
- Retrieve running-config or interface/address info from router2 to confirm 10.0.0.11 and that 172.16.0.0/24 is present/advertised.
- Run ping/traceroute from R1 toward 172.16.0.12 to validate end-to-end reachability.
